_comment: Source-of-truth payload for the `main` merge-queue requirement, as a GitHub repository RULESET.
Why a ruleset (not classic branch protection): classic `PUT .../branches/main/protection`
silently DROPS `required_merge_queue` (verified 2026-04-28) — which is why enabling the queue was
a manual UI step. The rulesets API DOES carry the queue: a `merge_queue` rule is REST-settable
(the 2026-04-28 test-repo experiment enabled the queue this way). So the queue toggle is now
config-as-code, killing the last manual step. See docs/spec/merge-queue-config.md.
Scope discipline: this ruleset carries ONLY the `merge_queue` rule. The required-status-checks
set stays in classic protection (`branch-protection.json`) — the 'rulesets for event-specific
required-checks lists' idea was tested and rejected (rulesets give no extra flexibility there).
The two layers are orthogonal: classic protection requires the checks; this ruleset requires the
queue. Together: a PR must pass checks AND merge through the queue.
Tuned for ~1-2 PR/day MVP cadence: queue depth ~= 0, fast feedback, low burn.
Re-tune if observed depth > 2 sustained or merge wait > 30 min.
ORG-ONLY: the `merge_queue` ruleset rule is an organization / GitHub-Team feature. Verified
against GitHub: this exact payload is accepted on an org repo (cogni-dao/*) and rejected (422)
on a personal-account repo. Operator-minted nodes live under the org owner, so this applies; a
node under a personal account is born queue-less (node formation skips the queue on 403/422).
Apply: bash infra/github/setup-main-branch.sh [<owner>/<repo>] (idempotent POST-or-PUT by name).
name: main-merge-queue
